stories.headline: SERVICENOW PLUNGES 18%, IBM DROPS 9% AS AI DISRUPTION FEARS HAMMER SOFTWARE SECTOR
stories.body: ServiceNow cratered ~18% and IBM fell ~9% after both reported earnings, with ServiceNow flagging delayed Middle East enterprise deals and investors questioning SaaS margin durability in an AI substitution environment. The S&P 500 Software & Services index shed more than 4%, dragging the S&P 500 down 0.41% to 7,108 and the Nasdaq down 0.89% to 24,439 — pulling back from Wednesday's record highs.

stories.headline: IRAN CEASEFIRE STALLS; BRENT CRUDE SURGES BACK ABOVE $100 AS VESSEL SEIZURES REPORTED
stories.body: Brent crude rose 3.5% to $105.27/bbl and WTI jumped 4.0% to $96.50 after reports of Iranian vessel seizures in the Strait of Hormuz with no diplomatic progress in Islamabad. The move reverses a large portion of the April 8 ceasefire-relief rally and is now directly hitting corporate guidance — ServiceNow explicitly cited Middle East uncertainty as a driver of delayed Q1 enterprise deals.

stories.headline: TEXAS INSTRUMENTS SURGES 18% ON DATA CENTER CHIP DEMAND; 81% OF S&P 500 BEATS ESTIMATES
stories.body: TI raised Q2 revenue and profit guidance above consensus on robust industrial and data center chip demand, surging 18% and lifting the broader semiconductor sector — the clearest sign yet that AI infrastructure spending remains intact even as application-layer software struggles. Of 87 S&P 500 companies reporting, 81% have beaten earnings estimates, but markets are ruthlessly punishing slowing growth or uncertain forward guidance.

stories.headline: APRIL 30 DOUBLE-HEADER: Q1 GDP AND MARCH PCE PRINT SIMULTANEOUSLY; STAGFLATION RISK RISING
stories.body: The BEA releases Q1 GDP and March PCE inflation together at 8:30 AM ET next Thursday — the most consequential dual data print of the year. Core PCE was last at 2.7%; with Brent near $105 and tariff pass-through accelerating, a hot inflation print alongside slowing growth would force markets to price a genuine stagflation scenario, pushing the first Fed cut even further off the table. FOMC meets May 5-6.

stories.headline: NCAA BASKETBALL TRANSFER PORTAL CLOSES TODAY; WASHINGTON, OTHER PROGRAMS FINALIZE 2026-27 ROSTERS
stories.body: The NCAA men's basketball transfer portal closed Thursday, ending the offseason roster-building window following Michigan's April 6 national championship (69-63 over UConn). Danny Sprinkle's Washington Huskies added four portal transfers — F LeJuan Watts (Texas Tech, 11.8 ppg/6.0 rpg), SG Parker Friedrichsen (Davidson), SF Steele Venters (Gonzaga), and PG Ryan Beasley (USF) — as top-five recruit Tyran Stokes cut UW from his final three in favor of Oregon, Kentucky, and Kansas.

stories.headline: SGA DROPS 37 AS THUNDER ROUT SUNS 120-107; OKC TAKES COMMANDING 2-0 FIRST-ROUND LEAD
stories.body: Shai Gilgeous-Alexander poured in 37 points as Oklahoma City dominated Phoenix 120-107 to seize a 2-0 series lead and cement themselves as the West's clear top seed. In the East, Detroit's Cade Cunningham (27 pts, 11 ast) powered the Pistons to a 98-83 Game 2 win over Orlando to even that series 1-1. Victor Wembanyama's availability for Game 3 remains uncertain following his second-quarter concussion in San Antonio's Game 2 loss to Portland.

stories.headline: NFL DRAFT OPENS IN PITTSBURGH; RAIDERS SELECT HEISMAN QB FERNANDO MENDOZA #1 OVERALL
stories.body: The 2026 NFL Draft kicked off Thursday night at Acrisure Stadium in Pittsburgh with Las Vegas taking Indiana Heisman winner Fernando Mendoza — who led the Hoosiers to their first-ever national championship — with the top pick. The Seattle Seahawks, reigning Super Bowl 60 champions holding the league-minimum four picks, are on the clock at #32 tonight; GM John Schneider has publicly signaled intent to trade back for additional Rounds 2-3 picks on Friday.

stories.headline: POST-SPRING QUESTIONS HAUNT CFP CONTENDERS; SPRING PORTAL WINDOW ELIMINATED FOR 2026
stories.body: ESPN flagged 11 CFP contenders — including Alabama, Georgia, Oregon, and BYU — entering summer with unresolved depth gaps exposed in spring practice. The NCAA's elimination of the spring football transfer window means programs cannot fill those holes until January 2027, making current spring evaluations unusually high-stakes for development and coaching. Washington's spring game, Dawgs After Dark, is set for May 1.

sports.detail: Beat Orlando Magic in Game 2, series tied 1-1. Cade Cunningham: 27 pts, 6 reb, 11 ast. Game 3 at Orlando Friday.
On the clock at pick #32 tonight in Pittsburgh (Super Bowl 60 champs). GM Schneider signals trade-back. Top needs: RB (Charbonnet ACL), CB. Rounds 2-3 Friday.
Dropped A's series finale 5-2 (Apr 22). Record 11-16. Off day today; new series begins Apr 24.
15 spring sessions underway through May 1. Dawgs After Dark spring game May 1. WR Jonah Coleman, CB Carver Willis in tonight's NFL Draft.
Portal closed today. Added F Watts (Texas Tech), G Friedrichsen (Davidson), F Venters (Gonzaga), G Beasley (USF) under coach Sprinkle.

watchlist: Q1 GDP advance + March PCE — Thursday Apr 30, 8:30 AM ET — core PCE last at 2.7%; hot print deepens Fed hold
FOMC meeting May 5-6 — market pricing hold through at least June; Brent near $105 narrows any cut window
NFL Draft Rounds 2-3 — Friday Apr 24 in Pittsburgh; Seahawks trade-back strategy pivotal with only 4 picks
Iran ceasefire talks — Islamabad negotiations stalled; sustained Brent above $100 = CPI upside risk
Mariners next series — team 11-16 starts new opponent Apr 24; rotation depth under scrutiny

deepDive.title: How AI Reprices SaaS Multiples: The NRR Mechanism Behind Today's Software Selloff
content: ServiceNow fell nearly 18% and IBM dropped 9% Thursday despite both reporting results roughly in line with consensus. The headline explanation — 'guidance uncertainty' — understates what actually happened in the market's calculus. Today's selloff is a textbook example of multiple compression triggered by a shift in long-run revenue quality assumptions, not just a near-term earnings miss. Understanding that distinction is central to analyzing any high-multiple growth sector, and it explains why a company can 'beat' and still lose a fifth of its market cap in a single session.

SaaS companies trade primarily on ARR growth and NRR (net dollar retention). NRR measures how existing customers spend year-over-year: if they expand contracts, NRR exceeds 100%; if they cut seats or churn, it falls below. Wall Street values SaaS at a premium EV/ARR multiple — ServiceNow traded near 18x — because high NRR implies compounding, sticky revenue. AI disruption directly threatens NRR: enterprises can now automate workflows that previously required expanding seat counts. When ServiceNow's guidance implied subscription growth slowing from ~23% to ~20%, the market didn't trim earnings by 3 percentage points. It re-rated the entire forward multiple, asking: if NRR structurally compresses over time, what is the correct terminal value?

Most observers miss the rule-of-40 dynamic embedded in buyside SaaS valuation. Rule-of-40 (revenue growth% + FCF margin%) is the primary screen analysts use to rank software names. ServiceNow at 60+ was in 'elite' territory commanding premium multiples. A guidance cut that shaves even 3-5 points off the growth component drops a company out of the top-tier bucket, triggering systematic re-allocation by quant funds that use rule-of-40 as a filter. The result is non-linear: a 3% growth miss can produce a 15-20% stock move because it crosses a classification threshold. The 2001 analog is instructive — Oracle fell 30%+ not because revenues collapsed but because the market re-underpriced terminal growth as the client-server software wave peaked.

For today's portfolio setup, the bifurcation is now explicit: Texas Instruments surged 18% on data center chip demand while ServiceNow fell 18% on AI substitution risk. Both carry 'AI' exposure in the popular narrative, but the market is pricing them through opposite lenses — TI as AI infrastructure enabler, ServiceNow as a potential AI target. If you're evaluating software sector exposure, the key discriminator is whether a company sits on the enabling layer (semis, hyperscalers, networking) or the application layer (workflow automation, CRM, collaboration) where AI substitution is most credible. The April 30 PCE/GDP print adds a second headwind: if growth slows while rates stay elevated, high-multiple software names face both discount rate expansion and growth premium compression simultaneously.
